title: A bending section for an endoscope
abstract: A bending section (5) for an endoscope (1) and the endoscope, where the bending section comprises a number of segments (12), where adjacent segments are connected by a first hinge (14) and a second hinge (24). Both the first and the second hinges are made integrally from the same material as the segments (12), and the first and second hinges are arranged for allowing the connected segments to bend in a bending plane. A second hinge at the distal end of a segment forms a continuous rib (44) with the second hinge (24) at the proximal end of the segment. This continuous rib is arranged perpendicular to the bending plane.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A bending section for an endoscope comprising a number of segments, where adjacent segments are connected by a first hinge and a second hinge, both the first and the second hinges are made integrally from the same material as the segments, the first and second hinges are arranged for allowing the connected segments to bend in a bending plane, wherein a second hinge at the distal end of a segment forms a continuous rib, perpendicular to the bending plane, with the second hinge at the proximal end of the segment. 2. The bending section according to claim 1, wherein a first width is defined for the first hinge in a direction perpendicular to the bending plane, and a second width is defined for the second hinge in a direction perpendicular to the bending plane, wherein the second width is at least a factor of two larger than the first width. 3. The bending section according to claim 1, wherein the first and second hinges are placed in mutual opposite or approximately opposite directions from a center axis of the bending section, and the first and second width are extending in a radial direction from the center axis. 4. The bending section according to claim 3, wherein a diameter line is defined for the bending section, the diameter passing through the center axis of the bending section, and the diameter line can be placed between two segments to pass both the first and the second hinge. 5. The bending section according to claim 1, wherein the bending section including segments and hinges is molded as a one-piece construction, preferably the material is a plastic material. 6. The bending section according to claim 1 or 2, comprising a passage through each segment, the passage being adapted to accommodate a working channel of the 

 endoscope, the passage being arranged such that it passes between the first hinge and the second hinge. 7. The bending section according to claim 2, wherein the second width is at least a factor of three larger than the first width, preferably the second width is a factor of four larger than the first width. 8. The bending section according to claim 2, wherein the second width varies as function of the position in a longitudinal direction from the distal end segment towards the proximal end segment. 9. The bending section according to claim 8, wherein the second width is smaller for a second hinge located close to either the distal end segment or the proximal end segment, compared to the second width of second hinges arranged between segments in the middle of the bending section. 10. The bending section according to claim 8, wherein a third hinge is arranged between at least some of the segments, and the third hinge is arranged between the first hinge and the second hinge. 11. The bending section according to any one of claims 1 - 7, wherein the second width of second hinges is the same or substantially the same between all segments of the bending section. 12. The bending section according to claim 1, wherein the second hinge separates two passages adapted for arrangement of electrical wires. 13. An endoscope comprising a bending section according to any one of the previous claims. 14. An endoscope system comprising a monitor and an endoscope according to claim 13.
